This exquisite selection of green teas is loaded with antioxidants that help detoxify the mind & body, support weight management and also help improve the cognitive functioning of your brain. 
The premium pyramid tea bags allow for better infusion. They're all unmatched in taste and quality and are packed with nutritional benefits. 
It's a sampler that's made for everyone and if you don't fall in love with it instantly, we're sure you'll grow to love it soon enough. 
Try out this perfectly curated box of wholesome goodness. It comes with 15 Delicious Flavors of Green Tea & we guarantee you'll want to come back for more:

column2: FG | Ginger Chocolate Herbal Tea Tisane, 30 Count (15x2)
column3:
Give into the perfect sweet indulgence, minus the guilt, with our Ginger Chocolate Herbal Tea. Earthy, woody flavors with the goodness of Rooibos Tea. You've got a sweet tooth and we got you sinful treats minus the sugar! Give in to the indulgence of our Ginger Chocolate Herbal Tea, which has all the best flavors of a decadent dessert, minus the usual calories and the heaviness. With earthy ginger, woody cinnamon, cocoa nibs, and the goodness of Rooibos tea - this blend is the perfect guilt-free treat for you. A non-caffeinated, vegan brew with delectable flavors and a world of good health.

 

The deep amber color liquor is full-bodied and starts with a decadent burst of chocolaty notes. The middle is marked by the sweet woody notes of Cinnamon and the earthy notes of Ginger mark the finish rounding off the cup wonderfully. One can experience undertones of honey, courtesy of the Rooibos that travel throughout the cup. Best consumed as a plain hot brew with no condiments.

column2: FG | Cookies & Cream Herbal Tea Tisane, 15 Count
column3:
Now sip on one of the most beloved, signature flavors of Cookies & Cream in a delicious herbal tea with Rooibos tea and spices!

If there was a match made in heaven, then you know that it was Chocolate chip cookies and creamy Vanilla. But at VAHDAM®, we know how to upgrade even the best of things and hence we bring you our rendition of the signature Cookies & Cream flavor with a touch of royalty courtesy of Kashmiri Saffron and Almonds to give you a rich mouthfeel. With the bountiful benefits of Rooibos Tea, this is heaven in a cup!

The cream-colored liquor has a medium body and starts with the heavy notes of cookies with hints of creamy vanilla. This delicious symphony is followed by the rich nutty notes of almond accentuated by the sweet and floral notes of saffron that travel throughout the cup. The overall cup is creamy, smooth, and sweet.Best consumed as a plain hot brew with no condiment, it's a complete treat in itself.

column2: FG | Berry Cinnamon Herbal Tea Tisane, 15 Count
column3:
A berrylicious fruity affair with the flavors of woody spices, flowers, and herbs - Our Berry Cinnamon is the perfect treat to turn the day around.

An alluring fruity infusion with an invigorating aroma of winter berries and sweet, spicy Cinnamon. A sincerely delicious ruby brew redolent of bountiful orchards, our Berry Cinnamon Herbal Tea is perfect for any season. A perfect full-bodied brew to warm you on chilly winter mornings or a quintessential summer iced cooler that can also be added to your favorite glass of wine. With a motley of exotic fruits and aromatic spices - this brew will win your heart in one sip!

The red liquor is full-bodied and starts with the subtle tart-like notes of Hibiscus which are in perfect harmony with the sprightly citrusy flavors of orange and lemon. The middle has the woody and sweet notes of Fennel and Cinnamon that run throughout the cup, uplifting the whole experience. The overall cup is citrusy and sweet. Best consumed hot and you can also add a dash of Vanilla extract to it to reveal more complex flavors.

column2: Castleton Moonlight Second Flush Black Tea (DJ 167/2023)
column3:
Castleton tea estate has an almost cult-like status amongst tea connoisseurs. This 130-year-old garden has an altitude of around 2000 metres. Amongst the best invoices of the season, this amazing lot of Castleton sets the benchmark for the most excellent Second Flush Darjeeling tea.

This prime Second Flush was harvested during the peak season - summer transitioning into monsoon. The lush, juicy leaves are full of goodness and sweetness. As you take the first sip, you taste the mellow sweetness with distant notes of grapes. column2: Giddapahar Classic Darjeeling First Flush Black Tea (DJ 11/2023)
column3:
Giddapahar means Eagle’s Cliff (Gidda = Eagle and Pahar = mountain or cliff) - a natural rock formation that overlooks the tea estate. It was started in 1881 where the combination of low temperatures and favourable slope create perfect conditions for excellent quality teas.

This early First flush invoice is grown at the higher elevation section of the estate. The leaves are lightly rolled and minimally processed to retain their natural fresheness and sweetness. The light-amber cup has a pleasant and smooth mouthfeel, with clear floral notes coming through. One can immediately taste the lush, verdant expanse of Darjeeling spring in the first sip, followed by lingering notes of sweet caramel intertwined with citrusy notes around the edges.

Every time you purchase a VAHDAM® India product, 1% of the revenue goes towards the education of our farmers’ children under the social initiative, ‘TEAch Me.’ In partnership with (RED), a minimum of $100,000 is donated by us to support the fight against pandemics like COVID & AIDS.

column2: Badamtam Premium Darjeeling First Flush Black Tea (DJ 10/2023)
column3:
The Badamtam tea estate is amongst the few tea gardens that overlook the breathtaking Himalayan mountain range with enchanting views of Mt. Kanchenjunga (the third highest mountain in the world), with its peak glistening in the golden hue of the sunrise.

This top invoice from the Badamtam bio-organic tea estate is made from the finest clonal bushes using the quintessential two leaves and a bud. The mellow-sweet tea brews into a lively and light cup with exceptional clarity. The taste is quite delicate and floral, with definite undertones of honey, intermingling with notes of ripe peaches and a hint of honey.

column2: Castleton Premium Darjeeling First Flush Black Tea (DJ 03/2023)
column3:
Castleton tea estate has an almost cult-like status amongst tea connoisseurs. This 130-year-old garden has an altitude of around 2000 metres. Amongst the best invoices of the season, this amazing lot of Castleton sets the benchmark for the most excellent First Flush Darjeeling tea.

Made from the select tender leaves and buds, the unmistakable sheen of bright green leaves dazzles you as it delights you with subtle hints of honey, cedar, and pistachios. The crispy clean cup has a silky smooth and honey-sweet mouthfeel, accentuated by dainty hyacinth and lily flavors amidst subtle notes of peach and almonds.

column2: Turmeric ginger latte
column3:
This 100% organic Turmeric Ginger Latte mix offers a delicious symphony of sweet and spiced flavors. The earthy-spiced flavors of Turmeric is given a spicy kick of Ginger. Black pepper brings a spicy zing to the cup while increasing Turmeric’s bio-absorption. The bitter notes in the cup are perfectly balanced by the natural zero-calorie sweetness of Stevia, rounding off the flavors. Simply add this to your preferred variant of nut milk or low-fat dairy milk to relish a creamy cup of natural goodness.

Every time you purchase a VAHDAM® India product, 1% of the revenue goes towards the education of our farmers’ children under the social initiative, ‘TEAch Me.’ In partnership with (RED), a minimum of $100,000 is donated by us to support the fight against pandemics like COVID & AIDS.

column2: Makaibari Premium Darjeeling Second Flush Black Tea (DJ 81/2021)
column3:
Makaibari is the world’s first tea factory, and is the only tea estate where tea is grown in complete harmony with tropical rain forests using biodynamic/permaculture practices. Makaibari has been an icon of Darjeeling teas for generations.

This outstanding Second Flush tea from Makabari was plucked on 16th June, when monsoon was at its peak. The cup is clean with a luscious and well-rounded mouthfeel. As you swirl the heavenly liquor, there is a distinct sweetness that fills your senses, which soon reveals the fresh flavour of summer flowers. The mid-palate has teasing and twining distinct nuances of citrus fruits, honey and dry-fruits. This excellent Second Flush tea should ideally be enjoyed in its purest form first - without milk or sugar.

column2: Test of Ashwagandha
column3:
A delectable symphony of timeless  bountiful Ayurvedic ingredients like calming Ashwagandha, bold Black Tea, and woody cinnamon to offer you a luscious earth cup of Chai. Our unique and flavorsome Organic Chai tea instant premix is infused with the  Ayurvedic adaptogen Ashwagandha, sweet and woody Cinnamon, and bold black tea to offer you a relaxing cup of wholesome goodness after a long day.  All natural flavors, only 45 calories, Vegan Chai to take along wherever you go!  

 A scrumptious latte, the cup has a light brown colour and opens with earthy notes of Ashwagandha which gets balanced by the sweetness of Cinnamon, undertones of which travel throughout the cup. The cup ends on a slight peppery note which gives it the kick. The overall cup is full bodied, earthy, and has a sweet flavor.

column2: Samabeong Summer Darjeeling Blossom Second Flush Black Tea (DJ 62/2022)
column3:
Samabeong is a high-altitude tea estate famous for its top-quality first and second flush teas. Established in 1882, Samabeong was famous of black bears (Sama = black, Beong = cave in the local Lepcha language). It has one of the best selection of clonal bushes, that, combined with slow-growth due to cold weather, result in sweet and intensely flavoured teas.

column2: Margaret's Hope 2022 Black Tea - Roses Tea
column3:
Famously known as Rosses Tea, this selection dates back to 153 years ago when these muscatel teas were planted bush by bush, sapling by sapling by the very first manager of the Margaret's Hope tea estate, Mr. CM Ross.

Original & heritage China bushes planted in the Rosses section of the estate spread across 4.5 hectares and it was in 1869 when the gardens were brimming with delicate leaves and buds that even today make exceptionally high-quality teas in the romantic and the very renowned Margaret's Hope tea estate in Darjeeling.

column2: Arya Diamond Darjeeling First Flush Black Tea (EX 30/2022)
column3:
Established in 1899, Goomtee tea estate is nestled between two of Darjeeling’s most famous tea estates - Jungpana and Castleton. The misty, cold and gentle sunshine results in teas that are mellow, brisk and bright with a soft and subtle flavour.

The tender leaves of Goomtee First Flush teas are left uncut and minimally processed to retain their natural sweetness and flavour. The delicately rolled, well-twisted leaves result in a bright green appearance with a nice sprinkling of fine, silvery tips. As you savour this in your mouth, subtle accents of hyacinth and honeysuckle are revealed, tapering off into a soothing finish that conjure images of vibrant, flowering crabapple trees.

column2: Giddapahar Classic Darjeeling First Flush Black Tea (DJ 15/2022)
column3:
Giddapahar means Eagle’s Cliff (Gidda = Eagle and Pahar = mountain or cliff) - a natural rock formation that overlooks the tea estate. It was started in 1881 where the combination of low temperatures and favourable slope create perfect conditions for excellent quality teas.

This early First flush invoice is grown at the higher elevation section of the estate. The leaves are lightly rolled and minimally processed to retain their natural fresheness and sweetness. The light-amber cup has a pleasant and smooth mouthfeel, with clear floral notes coming through. One can immediately taste the lush, verdant expanse of Darjeeling spring in the first sip, followed by lingering notes of sweet caramel intertwined with citrusy notes around the edges.

column2: Runglee Rungliot Premium Darjeeling First Flush Black Tea (DJ 05/2022)
column3:
Runglee Rungliot is one of the oldest tea estates tracing its origin back to 1858. Legend has it, that a Buddhist monk was roaming the Himalayas in search of a perfect cup of tea, until one day, he reached a scenic spot in the Teesta valley of Darjeeling, Runglee Rungliot.

It is a heady mix of twisted black and green leaves interspersed with delicate silver tips that give a fresh, nutty aroma. When brewed, the cup is mild-gold-green, crisp, and clean. The taste is mild and mellow, with a smooth and sweet mouthfeel. Around the edges, clear notes of peony and freesia entice you amidst the sweetness of honeycomb. As you finish, undertones of toasted nuts and juicy tangerines linger.

Truly Artisanal, the leaves were hand-rolled under the careful supervision of experienced tea at an elevation of 1,900 meters.


The two leaves and a bud are starkly noticeable. That is how delicately these teas are ‘hand-made’- and that is what makes them so rare and artisanal. A brilliant work of nature: emerald green and silver. You are sure to be transported to the misty blue mountains of the Nilgiris. The leaves brew into a crystal clear cup with the tint of rare pearls. The first sip is crisp and clean, floral and fragrant. As you savor this tea, delicate and complex flavors engulf your mouth- there is a sweetness of wild honey intermingling with the soothing tones of lavender. Ends with hints of scented almonds that surprise you.

Sweetness: Medium Caffeine: Medium Time of the day: Anytime Benefits: Enhanced Alertness

column3:
A berrylicious fruity affair with the flavors of woody spices, flowers, and herbs - Our Berry Cinnamon is the perfect treat to turn the day around.

An alluring fruity infusion with an invigorating aroma of winter berries and sweet, spicy Cinnamon. A sincerely delicious ruby brew redolent of bountiful orchards, our Berry Cinnamon Herbal Tea is perfect for any season. A perfect full-bodied brew to warm you on chilly winter mornings or a quintessential summer iced cooler that can also be added to your favorite glass of wine. With a motley of exotic fruits and aromatic spices - this brew will win your heart in one sip!

The red liquor is full-bodied and starts with the subtle tart-like notes of Hibiscus which are in perfect harmony with the sprightly citrusy flavors of orange and lemon. The middle has the woody and sweet notes of Fennel and Cinnamon that run throughout the cup, uplifting the whole experience. The overall cup is citrusy and sweet. Best consumed hot and you can also add a dash of Vanilla extract to it to reveal more complex flavors.

Makaibari is the world’s first tea factory, and is the only tea estate where tea is grown in complete harmony with tropical rain forests using biodynamic/permaculture practices. Makaibari has been an icon of Darjeeling teas for generations.

This outstanding Second Flush tea from Makabari was plucked on 16th June, when monsoon was at its peak. The cup is clean with a luscious and well-rounded mouthfeel. As you swirl the heavenly liquor, there is a distinct sweetness that fills your senses, which soon reveals the fresh flavour of summer flowers. The mid-palate has teasing and twining distinct nuances of citrus fruits, honey and dry-fruits. This excellent Second Flush tea should ideally be enjoyed in its purest form first - without milk or sugar.

column3:
The Badamtam tea estate is amongst the few tea gardens that overlook the breathtaking Himalayan mountain range with enchanting views of Mt. Kanchenjunga (the third highest mountain in the world), with its peak glistening in the golden hue of the sunrise.

This top invoice from the Badamtam bio-organic tea estate is made from the finest clonal bushes using the quintessential two leaves and a bud. The mellow-sweet tea brews into a lively and light cup with exceptional clarity. The taste is quite delicate and floral, with definite undertones of honey, intermingling with notes of ripe peaches and a hint of honey.

Every time you purchase a VAHDAM® India product, 1% of the revenue goes towards the education of our farmers’ children under the social initiative, ‘TEAch Me.’ In partnership with (RED), a minimum of $100,000 is donated by us to support the fight against pandemics like COVID & AIDS.

column2: Rohini Classic Darjeeling Second Flush Black Tea (DJ 76/2021)
column3:
The youngest tea estate in Darjeeling, Rohini tea estate is located in the Kurseong valley of Darjeeling. It is more than 100 years old today, and has the originally planted China bushes, producing the highly sought-after second flush muscatel teas.

Plucked in the last week of May, the leaves are coppery brown with golden tips. When brewed, they give a bright, amber-orange cup that is crisp and vibrant. The mouthfeel is sweet with clear notes of tropical fruits, from which emerges another delightful muscatel layer that is finally rounded off with honeycomb sweetness.

Every time you purchase a VAHDAM® India product, 1% of the revenue goes towards the education of our farmers’ children under the social initiative, ‘TEAch Me.’ In partnership with (RED), a minimum of $100,000 is donated by us to support the fight against pandemics like COVID & AIDS.

column2: Giddapahar Classic Darjeeling Second Flush Black Tea (DJ 80/2022)
column3:
Giddapahar means Eagle’s Cliff (Gidda = Eagle and Pahar = mountain or cliff) - a natural rock formation that overlooks the tea estate. It was started in 1881 where the combination of low temperatures and favourable slope create perfect conditions for excellent quality teas.

The full-bloom, copper-black leaves have a subtle toasty-floral aroma. When brewed, the golden cup is sweet and clean. The well-rounded mellow cup immediately bursts into layers of complex fruity flavour at the first sip, followed by hints of woody notes at the mid-palate, finishing off with a lingering muscatel hues.

column2: Gopaldhara Darjeeling Second Flush Black Tea (DJ 148/2021)
column3:
Gopal is another name of Lord Krishna and Dhara refers to the many natural streams flowing through the garden. Hence, the name. Gopaldhara prides itself in making ‘Pure Tea, Fit for the Gods’. Spread across lush 320 hectares, Gopaldhara produces some of the finest Darjeeling Teas.

Carefully hand-plucked early morning by skilled workers from an elevation of 5000-6000 ft., these teas are meticulously processed and lovingly supervised by experts. The rustic black-brown leaves with tips brew into a mellow and crisp golden cup. As you swirl it in your mouth, the honey-sweet flavour enveloped with layers of tropical fruits comes to the fore.

Every time you purchase a VAHDAM® India product, 1% of the revenue goes towards the education of our farmers’ children under the social initiative, ‘TEAch Me.’ In partnership with (RED), a minimum of $100,000 is donated by us to support the fight against pandemics like COVID & AIDS.
